meta.prototype_note: PROTOTYPE — Live API connections not yet active. Data from manual WebSearch scan.

executive_summary: This week the AI industry produced a courtroom, a union ballot, 8,000 layoffs, and a compute deal between Elon Musk's company and a company Elon Musk called a threat to civilisation — all in the same five days. The theme is not capability or progress; it is the widening gap between what AI companies say they are and what they are actually doing. OpenAI's safety commitments are being examined under oath for the first time. DeepMind researchers voted 98% to unionise not over wages but over four words in a Pentagon contract. Meta announced workforce cuts and simultaneously raised its AI infrastructure spending by $73 billion — and used the phrase 'reallocation toward AI' deliberately, not 'cost reduction.' Meanwhile, Anthropic's CEO issued a public warning that his own model is dangerous enough to require restricted access, and a 15,000-domain AI scam network was documented running infrastructure that standard security tools cannot detect. The gap between the AI industry's public narrative and its actual decisions has rarely been this visible in a single week.

stories.headline: The OpenAI trial is about safety, not Elon Musk
stories.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial entered week two with testimony that product pressure compromised safety work at OpenAI — a detail buried under the personal drama dominating coverage.

stories.headline: Anthropic's CEO warns of a 'moment of danger' — his own model found tens of thousands of vulnerabilities
stories.summary: Dario Amodei warned publicly that Mythos found nearly 300 Firefox vulnerabilities (vs 20 from the previous model) and tens of thousands across all major software. Access is restricted to vetted partners under Project Glasswing.

stories.headline: Anthropic signs compute deal with xAI — months after Musk called Anthropic a civilization threat
stories.summary: Anthropic will use all 300MW and 220,000 GPUs of xAI's Colossus 1 data center. Musk called Anthropic a company that 'hates Western civilization' in February. He is currently in trial against OpenAI for AI safety concerns.

stories.headline: DeepMind UK workers vote 98% to unionize — over one clause in a Pentagon contract
stories.summary: Workers at DeepMind's London HQ voted 98% to form a union, citing the Google-Pentagon deal clause 'for any lawful purpose.' They demand restoration of Google's scrapped weapons pledge and an independent ethics body. Management has 10 working days to respond.

stories.headline: Meta cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 while raising AI capex to $145 billion — framed as 'reallocation'
stories.summary: Meta will cut 10% of its workforce on May 20 and raise 2026 AI infrastructure spending from $72B to $125-145B. The company is explicit: this is reallocation toward AI, not cost reduction. Teams are reorganised into AI-focused 'pods.'

stories.headline: Meta deploys AI that scans height and bone structure to find underage users — says it's not facial recognition
stories.summary: Meta will analyse physical signals across all user content to detect under-13 accounts. The company emphasises 'not facial recognition.' Critics raise questions about biometric profiling of all users to identify some, accuracy across demographic groups, and consent.

stories.headline: OpenAI closes $10 billion PE joint venture to embed engineers inside businesses — Palantir-style
stories.summary: The Deployment Company, a $10B JV with TPG and 17 other PE firms, will embed OpenAI engineers directly inside client businesses in healthcare, shipping, manufacturing, and finance. PE investors receive a guaranteed 17.5% annual return.

stories.headline: Microsoft may abandon its 2030 carbon-free energy pledge as AI data centers add a gigawatt every three months
stories.summary: Bloomberg reports Microsoft is weighing whether to delay or abandon its commitment to 100% renewable hourly energy by 2030. The company adds a gigawatt of data center capacity every quarter. Microsoft denies it while spending $190B on data centers through December.

stories.headline: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant is now the ChatGPT default — it claims 52% fewer hallucinations, self-measured
stories.summary: GPT-5.5 Instant replaces GPT-5.3 Instant as the default ChatGPT model. OpenAI claims 52.5% fewer hallucinations on medical, legal, and financial prompts — measured by OpenAI, on prompts OpenAI selected.

stories.headline: Anthropic's $1.5 billion PE venture with Goldman and Blackstone — announced within minutes of OpenAI's parallel deal
stories.summary: Anthropic partnered with Goldman Sachs, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman for a $1.5B enterprise AI JV — announced within minutes of OpenAI's $10B Deployment Company on May 4. Both target the same sectors.

stories.headline: US government signs AI pre-testing deals with Google, Microsoft, and xAI — no enforcement mechanism published
stories.summary: NIST's Center for AI Standards signed agreements to evaluate pre-release models from Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI for security. The agreements are voluntary with no published consequence for non-compliance or failed testing.

stories.headline: Microsoft offers its first voluntary retirement programme in 51 years as AI spending tightens other budgets
stories.summary: Microsoft offered roughly 8,750 U.S. employees a voluntary buyout with a June 8 deadline — the first such programme in its 51-year history. The move follows accelerating AI capex and reported cuts in non-AI divisions.

stories.headline: Federal court pauses Colorado's AI Act — DOJ argues state AI regulation is unconstitutional compelled speech
stories.summary: A federal court stayed Colorado SB 24-205 on April 27. xAI filed the challenge; the DOJ intervened to argue the law 'impermissibly compels AI systems to adopt state-defined viewpoints' — a legal theory that could block most state AI regulation.

stories.headline: Researchers expose 15,500-domain AI deepfake investment scam network that hides from security scanners
stories.summary: Malwarebytes documented a fraud operation spanning 15,500+ domains using Keitaro ad-tracking as a cloaking layer — victims see deepfake celebrity investment pitches, security scanners see harmless pages. Generative AI produces campaign material at scale across multiple languages.

stories.headline: OpenAI and Anthropic both restrict their cybersecurity AI models to vetted professionals the same week
stories.summary: OpenAI began distributing GPT-5.5-Cyber to restricted security teams, days after Anthropic restricted Mythos under Project Glasswing. Both labs built specialised security models; both decided independently not to release them openly.

top_video_recommendation.title: The OpenAI trial is about safety, not Elon Musk
top_video_recommendation.rationale: Highest score this week. The trial is active and producing testimony right now — the window closes once the verdict is in. Every other outlet is covering the personal drama; nobody has made the safety testimony the centre of the story. That gap is exactly where this channel operates.
